name: Fixed safety/filter classifier submodels
baseline_comparison: Programmable accelerators are strong, but isolation and fallback make fixed or semi-fixed safety filters testable.
rationale: Small repeated classifiers can be long-lived, isolated, and tolerant of conservative fallback.
falsifier: Demote if policy churn forces weekly updates or false-positive/negative cost requires full programmable execution.

name: Small always-on wake-word or router models
baseline_comparison: Programmable accelerator cost may dominate for always-on tiny models; fixed local logic has a plausible idle-energy niche.
rationale: Always-on routing has stable weights, simple interfaces, and high aggregate reuse despite small per-request work.
falsifier: Demote if the router must track fast-changing model menus or if CPU/NPU idle states already match the energy target.

name: Repeated retrieval/reranking feature transforms
baseline_comparison: Software caching helps, but repeated feature transforms can remain hot across requests.
rationale: Reranking pipelines often reuse stable transforms at high volume and can fall back to programmable paths.
falsifier: Demote if workload-specific feature changes dominate or batching/caching erases memory movement.

name: Mixture-of-experts router or dispatch logic
baseline_comparison: Software runtimes already optimize dispatch, so credit only the stable high-volume router core.
rationale: Routers are reused heavily and smaller than experts, but integration with dynamic serving is nontrivial.
falsifier: Demote if expert churn or load balancing changes the router distribution faster than quarterly.

name: Positional encoding / RoPE / deterministic transform support
baseline_comparison: Deterministic transforms are easy to optimize in software or programmable logic; physicalized weights add little.
rationale: This is stable and reusable, but it is not obviously weight-dominated.
falsifier: Demote if profiling shows negligible memory/energy share versus attention and KV movement.

name: Domain-specific adapter or LoRA blocks with slow update cadence
baseline_comparison: The software-optimized baseline is dangerous here because adapters are already compact and easy to swap.
rationale: Some long-lived enterprise adapters have enough stability to justify semi-fixed treatment, not permanent masks.
falsifier: Demote if tenant-specific updates are monthly or if memory savings from adapter caching reaches 50%.

name: Fixed or slowly changing embedding tables for constrained vocabularies/domains
baseline_comparison: Programmable memory hierarchy improvements compete directly; only constrained stable vocabularies survive.
rationale: Constrained domains can make table storage stable and reusable, but vocabulary churn is a major risk.
falsifier: Demote if vocabulary/logit head variants churn or if compression/caching removes the movement bottleneck.

name: Quantization/dequantization and scaling constants
baseline_comparison: Quantization is already a software/compiler strength, so fixed constants alone get limited credit.
rationale: Constants are stable and easy to integrate, but energy upside may be too small for a dedicated substrate.
falsifier: Demote if compiler fusion removes standalone movement or if formats change per model family.

name: Frozen first-layer projections or low-rank projection blocks
baseline_comparison: Programmable accelerators handle dense projections well, so physicalization must exploit exceptional reuse or locality.
rationale: Projection blocks are static and simple, but they are deeply coupled to model layout.
falsifier: Demote if accelerator matrix units keep the block bandwidth-local or if model revisions alter dimensions.

name: KV-cache compression/decompression transforms
baseline_comparison: Software/runtime KV management is a core null-hypothesis strength; fixed transforms need robust cross-model reuse.
rationale: KV traffic is important, but context-dependent behavior makes fixed physicalization risky.
falsifier: Demote if quality loss is prompt-dependent or runtime compression policies change frequently.

name: Full frontier LLM dense weights burned permanently into fixed logic
baseline_comparison: Even if per-request energy looks attractive, update cadence, yield, stranded capital, and programmable baselines dominate.
rationale: Superficially large energy upside fails the null because the entire model is high-churn and hard to repair.
falsifier: Promote only with evidence of multi-year frozen weights, extreme volume, repairability, and no competitive programmable path.

name: Frequently updated tenant-specific fine-tunes or adapters
baseline_comparison: Software adapter loading and routing are designed for this case; fixed substrates strand value quickly.
rationale: Update churn prevents amortization even if adapters are small.
falsifier: Promote only if a tenant model freezes for quarters and reaches high shared reuse.

name: High-churn vocabulary/logit-head variants
baseline_comparison: Tokenizer/head churn is exactly where software indirection and table updates are cheap.
rationale: Variants look table-like but update and SKU fragmentation erase the fixed-weight case.
falsifier: Promote only for a constrained vocabulary with stable deployment and high shared traffic.

name: Dynamic attention over live context as fixed physical logic
baseline_comparison: KV/cache software and programmable accelerators are the right baseline; live context is not static weight state.
rationale: Attention dataflow is important but too dynamic to burn as fixed weights.
falsifier: Promote only for a narrow fixed transform inside attention with measured stability and fallback.

name: Training/optimizer state and gradient computation
baseline_comparison: This is not inference weight reuse; programmable training systems remain the relevant substrate.
rationale: Optimizer state mutates continuously and cannot amortize fixed physicalization.
falsifier: Promote only if the scope changes to inference-only frozen auxiliary weights.
